_publications._publicationTitle: Neurofibromatosis type 1-dependent alterations in mouse microglia function are not cell-intrinsic.
_publications._context: CX3CR1-CreER [ 71 ] were intercrossed with Nf1 flox/flox [ 1 ] | R26 tdTom/tdTom; Prss56 Cre/Cre, R26 tdTom/tdTom, Nf1 flox/flox; Nf1 flox/flox; Nf1 +/− | Nf1 WT (Nf1^+/+, Nf1^fl/fl or Nf1^fl/+) and heterozygous Nf1-mutant (Nf1^+/mut or Nf1^fl/mut) mice were generated previously | All the CKO mice have genotypes of Cre+; Nf1 flox/flox | Nf1 flox/flox (Zhu et al. 2001) and Nf1 GFAP CKO (Bajenaru et al. 2002) mice were generated as previously described and maintained on a C57Bl/6 background | crossing 2.3-kb α1(I) collagen-cre Nf1 flox/flox mice and Nf1 flox/flox mice | Nf1^fl/fl, OPG Nf1^fl/mut (Nf1^+/-) and Nf1 (wild-type) mice were previously generated | Nf1flox/flox mice were provided by Dr. Luis Parada at the University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center

_publications._publicationTitle: Unraveling the development of cutaneous neurofibromas in neurofibromatosis type 1.
Nf1 mutation disrupts activity-dependent oligodendroglial plasticity and motor learning in mice.
Transient inhibition of the ERK pathway prevents cerebellar developmental defects and improves long-term motor functions in murine models of neurofibromatosis type 1.
Microarray analyses reveal regional astrocyte heterogeneity with implications for neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1)-regulated glial proliferation.
Local low-dose lovastatin delivery improves the bone-healing defect caused by Nf1 loss of function in osteoblasts.
NF1 mutation drives neuronal activity-dependent initiation of optic glioma.
The haploinsufficient hematopoietic microenvironment is critical to the pathological fracture repair in murine models of neurofibromatosis type 1.
